metadata.preview:
# AgentCube CLI (cli-toolkit) Specification

## Purpose

Command-line tool (`kubectl-agentcube`) for packaging, building, publishing, invoking, and checking status of AI agents against AgentCube or standard Kubernetes.

## Requirements

### Requirement: Global CLI options
The system SHALL expose a Typer application named `agentcube` with global options on the root callback.

#### Scenario: Version display
- **GIVEN** the CLI is installed and importable
- **WHEN** the user passes `--version` (eager option, `Optional[bool]`, default `None`)
- **THEN** the CLI SHALL print `AgentCube CLI (kubectl agentcube) version: {__version__}` (Rich markup) and exit without running a subcommand (`typer.Exit()`), where `__version__` is `0.1.0` from `agentcube.__init__`

#### Scenario: Global verbose logging
- **GIVEN** the user passes `--verbose` / `-v` (`bool`, default `False`) on the root callback
- **WHEN** the callback runs
- **THEN** the system SHALL call `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)`

<content>1: # AgentCube Makefile
2: 
3: # Variables
4: GO ?= go
5: GOLINT ?= golangci-lint
6: GOFLAGS ?= $(GOFLAGS:)
7: DOCKER ?= docker
8: HELM ?= helm
9: VERSION ?= latest
10: REGISTRY ?= ghcr.io/$(USER)
11: WORKLOADMANAGER_IMAGE ?= workloadmanager
12: ROUTER_IMAGE ?= router
13: PICOD_IMAGE ?= picod
14: 
15: # Go build settings
16: GO_BUILD_FLAGS := -ldflags="-s -w"
17: CGO_ENABLED := 0
18: 
19: # Default target
20: all: generate build
21: 
22: .PHONY: all
23: all: generate build
24: 
25: # Build targets
26: .PHONY: build
27: build:
28: 	@echo "Building workload manager..."
29: 	@$(GO) build $(GO_BUILD_FLAGS) -o bin/workloadmanager ./cmd/workload-manager
30: 	@echo "Building router..."
31: 	@$(GO) build $(GO_BUILD_FLAGS) -o bin/router ./cmd/router
32: 	@echo "Building picod..."
33: 	@$(GO) build $(GO_BUILD_FLAGS) -o bin/picod ./cmd/picod
34: 	@echo "Building agentd..."
35: 	@$(GO) build $(GO_BUILD_FLAGS) -o bin/agentd ./cmd/agentd
36: 
37: .PHONY: clean
38: clean:
39: 	@echo "Cleaning..."
40: 	@rm -rf bin/
41: 
42: # Code generation
43: .PHONY: generate
44: generate: gen-crd gen-client
45: 
46: .PHONY: gen-crd
47: gen-crd:
48: 	@echo "Generating CRDs..."
49: 	@$(GO) run sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen@latest \
50: 		crd:trivialVersions=true,preserveUnknownFields=false \
51: 		rbac:roleName=manager-role \
52: 		webhook \
53: 		paths="./pkg/apis/..." \
54: 		output:crd:artifacts:config=manifests/charts/base/crds
55: 
56: .PHONY: gen-client
57: gen-client:
58: 	@echo "Generating clientset..."
59: 	@$(GO) run k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/client-gen@latest \
60: 		--input-base="github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/apis" \
61: 		--input=runtime/v1alpha1 \
62: 		--clientset-name=versioned \
63: 		--output-base=. \
64: 		--output-package=github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/client/clientset
65: 
66: .PHONY: gen-all
67: gen-all: gen-crd gen-client
68: 
69: .PHONY: gen-check
70: gen-check: gen-all
71: 	@git diff --exit-code
72: 
73: # Test targets
74: .PHONY: test
75: test:
76: 	@echo "Running tests..."
77: 	@$(GO) test -race -v ./pkg/...
78: 
79: .PHONY: test-coverage
80: test-coverage:
81: 	@$(GO) test -race -v -coverprofile=coverage.out -coverpkg=./pkg/... ./pkg/...
82: 
83: # Lint
84: .PHONY: lint
85: lint:
86: 	@echo "Running linter..."
87: 	@$(GOLINT) run --config .golangci.yml ./...
88: 
89: # Docker targets
90: .PHONY: docker-build
91: docker-build:
92: 	@echo "Building Docker images..."
93: 	@$(DOCKER) build -f docker/Dockerfile -t $(REGISTRY)/$(WORKLOADMANAGER_IMAGE):$(VERSION) .
94: 	@$(DOCKER) build -f docker/Dockerfile.router -t $(REGISTRY)/$(ROUTER_IMAGE):$(VERSION) .
95: 	@$(DOCKER) build -f docker/Dockerfile.picod -t $(REGISTRY)/$(PICOD_IMAGE):$(VERSION) .
96: 
97: .PHONY: docker-push
98: docker-push:
99: 	@echo "Pushing Docker images..."
100: 	@$(DOCKER) push $(REGISTRY)/$(WORKLOADMANAGER_IMAGE):$(VERSION)
101: 	@$(DOCKER) push $(REGISTRY)/$(ROUTER_IMAGE):$(VERSION)
102: 	@$(DOCKER) push $(REGISTRY)/$(PICOD_IMAGE):$(VERSION)
103: 
104: .PHONY: docker-buildx-push
105: docker-buildx-push:
106: 	@echo "Building and pushing multi-arch images..."
107: 	@$(DOCKER) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
108: 		-f docker/Dockerfile -t $(REGISTRY)/$(WORKLOADMANAGER_IMAGE):$(VERSION) --push .
109: 	@$(DOCKER) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
110: 		-f docker/Dockerfile.router -t $(REGISTRY)/$(ROUTER_IMAGE):$(VERSION) --push .
111: 	@$(DOCKER) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
112: 		-f docker/Dockerfile.picod -t $(REGISTRY)/$(PICOD_IMAGE):$(VERSION) --push .
113: 
114: # E2E tests
115: .PHONY: e2e
116: e2e:
117: 	@echo "Running E2E tests..."
118: 	@test/e2e/run_e2e.sh
119: 
120: .PHONY: e2e-clean
121: e2e-clean:
122: 	@echo "Cleaning E2E environment..."
123: 	@test/e2e/cleanup.sh
124: 
125: # Copyright
126: .PHONY: gen-copyright
127: gen-copyright:
128: 	@find . -name "*.go" -not -path "./vendor/*" | while read f; do \
129: 		if ! grep -q "Copyright" "$$f"; then \
130: 			echo "Adding copyright to $$f"; \
131: 			echo -e "// Copyright $(shell date +%Y) Volcano Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n" | cat - "$$f" | sponge "$$f"; \
132: 		fi \
133: 	done

36: 	"k8s.io/klog/v2"

38: 	"github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/api"
39: 	"github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/apis/runtime/v1alpha1"
40: 	"github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/store"
41: )
42: 
43: const (
44: 	DefaultSandboxIdleTimeout = 15 * time.Minute
45: 	DefaultReadTimeout        = 15 * time.Second
46: 	DefaultIdleTimeout        = 90 * time.Second
47: )
48: 
49: var (
50: 	port       string
51: 	enableTLS  bool
52: 	tlsCert    string
53: 	tlsKey     string
54: 	enableAuth bool
55: 	debug      bool
56: )
57: 
58: func init() {
59: 	flag.StringVar(&port, "port", "8080", "Server port")
60: 	flag.BoolVar(&enableTLS, "enable-tls", false, "Enable TLS")
61: 	flag.StringVar(&tlsCert, "tls-cert", "", "TLS certificate path")
62: 	flag.StringVar(&tlsKey, "tls-key", "", "TLS key path")
63: 	flag.BoolVar(&enableAuth, "enable-auth", false, "Enable authentication")
64: 	flag.BoolVar(&debug, "debug", false, "Enable debug mode")
65: }
66: 
67: func main() {
68: 	flag.Parse()
69: 
70: 	if debug {
71: 		gin.SetMode(gin.DebugMode)
72: 	} else {
73: 		gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	// Initialize store
77: 	storage := store.Storage()
78: 
79: 	// Ping store
80: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
81: 	defer cancel()
82: 	if err := storage.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
83: 		klog.Fatalf("Failed to ping store: %v", err)
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	// Create Kubernetes client
87: 	config, err := rest.InClusterConfig()
88: 	if err != nil {
89: 		klog.Fatalf("Failed to get in-cluster config: %v", err)
90: 	}
91: 
92: 	dynamicClient, err := dynamic.NewForConfig(config)
93: 	if err != nil {
94: 		klog.Fatalf("Failed to create dynamic client: %v", err)
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	// Create HTTP server
98: 	engine := gin.New()
99: 	engine.Use(gin.Recovery())
100: 
101: 	// Health endpoint
102: 	engine.GET("/health", func(c *gin.Context) {
103: 		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "healthy"})
104: 	})
105: 
106: 	// V1 API routes with middleware
107: 	v1 := engine.Group("/v1")
108: 	v1.Use(loggingMiddleware())
109: 	if enableAuth {
110: 		v1.Use(authMiddleware())
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	// Agent runtime routes
114: 	v1.POST("/agent-runtime", createSandboxHandler(dynamicClient, storage, "AgentRuntime"))
115: 	v1.DELETE("/agent-runtime/sessions/:sessionId", deleteSessionHandler(dynamicClient, storage))
116: 
117: 	// Code interpreter routes
118: 	v1.POST("/code-interpreter", createSandboxHandler(dynamicClient, storage, "CodeInterpreter"))
119: 	v1.DELETE("/code-interpreter/sessions/:sessionId", deleteSessionHandler(dynamicClient, storage))
120: 
121: 	// Start garbage collection
122: 	go startGarbageCollector(storage, dynamicClient, config)
123: 
124: 	// Create HTTP server
125: 	server := &http.Server{
126: 		Addr:              ":" + port,
127: 		Handler:           h2c.NewHandler(engine, &http2.Server{}),
128: 		ReadTimeout:       DefaultReadTimeout,
129: 		IdleTimeout:       DefaultIdleTimeout,
130: 		ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second,
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	// Start server in goroutine
134: 	go func() {
135: 		klog.Infof("Starting workload manager on port %s", port)
136: 		if err := server.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
137: 			klog.Fatalf("Failed to start server: %v", err)
138: 		}
139: 	}()
140: 
141: 	// Wait for interrupt signal
142: 	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
143: 	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
144: 	<-quit
145: 
146: 	klog.Info("Shutting down server...")
147: 
148: 	// Graceful shutdown
149: 	ctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 15*time.Second)
150: 	defer cancel()
151: 	if err := server.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
152: 		klog.Errorf("Server shutdown error: %v", err)
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	// Close store
156: 	if err := storage.Close(); err != nil {
157: 		klog.Errorf("Failed to close store: %v", err)
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	klog.Info("Server stopped")
161: }
162: 
163: func createSandboxHandler(dynamicClient dynamic.Interface, storage store.Store, kind string) gin.HandlerFunc {
164: 	return func(c *gin.Context) {
165: 		var req api.CreateSandboxRequest
166: 		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
167: 			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"message": "Invalid request body"})
168: 			return
169: 		}
170: 
171: 		req.Kind = kind
172: 		if err := req.Validate(); err != nil {
173: 			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"message": err.Error()})
174: 			return
175: 		}
176: 
177: 		ctx := c.Request.Context()
178: 
179: 		// Create sandbox via dynamic client
180: 		var gvr schema.GroupVersionResource
181: 		if kind == "CodeInterpreter" {
182: 			gvr = v1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("codeinterpreters")
183: 		} else {
184: 			gvr = v1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("agentruntimes")
185: 		}
186: 
187: 		// Store placeholder
188: 		sessionID := generateSessionID()
189: 		sandboxInfo := &store.SandboxInfo{
190: 			SessionID: sessionID,
191: 			Name:      req.Name,
192: 			Namespace: req.Namespace,
193: 			Kind:      kind,
194: 			CreatedAt: time.Now(),
195: 			ExpiresAt: time.Now().Add(DefaultSandboxIdleTimeout),
196: 		}
197: 
198: 		if err := storage.StoreSandbox(ctx, sandboxInfo); err != nil {
199: 			klog.Errorf("Failed to store sandbox: %v", err)
200: 			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"message": "internal server error"})
201: 			return
202: 		}
203: 
204: 		// TODO: Actually create sandbox resource in Kubernetes
205: 		// This is a simplified implementation
206: 
207: 		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, api.CreateSandboxResponse{
208: 			SessionID:   sessionID,
209: 			SandboxID:   sessionID,
210: 			SandboxName: req.Name,
211: 			EntryPoints: []api.EntryPoint{},
212: 		})
213: 	}
214: }
215: 
216: func deleteSessionHandler(dynamicClient dynamic.Interface, storage store.Store) gin.HandlerFunc {
217: 	return func(c *gin.Context) {
218: 		sessionID := c.Param("sessionId")
219: 		if sessionID == "" {
220: 			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"message": "session ID is required"})
221: 			return
222: 		}
223: 
224: 		ctx := c.Request.Context()
225: 
226: 		// Load sandbox from store
227: 		sandbox, err := storage.GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID)
228: 		if err == store.ErrNotFound {
229: 			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"message": "Session ID " + sessionID + " not found, maybe already deleted"})
230: 			return
231: 		}
232: 		if err != nil {
233: 			klog.Errorf("Failed to get sandbox: %v", err)
234: 			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"message": "internal server error"})
235: 			return
236: 		}
237: 
238: 		// TODO: Delete actual Kubernetes resources
239: 
240: 		// Delete from store
241: 		if err := storage.DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID); err != nil {
242: 			klog.Errorf("Failed to delete sandbox from store: %v", err)
243: 		}
244: 
245: 		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"message": "Sandbox deleted successfully"})
246: 	}
247: }
248: 
249: func loggingMiddleware() gin.HandlerFunc {
250: 	return func(c *gin.Context) {
251: 		start := time.Now()
252: 		c.Next()
253: 		klog.Infof("%s %s %d %v", c.Request.Method, c.Request.URL.Path, c.Writer.Status(), time.Since(start))
254: 	}
255: }
256: 
257: func authMiddleware() gin.HandlerFunc {
258: 	return func(c *gin.Context) {
259: 		auth := c.GetHeader("Authorization")
260: 		if auth == "" {
261: 			c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"message": "Missing authorization header"})
262: 			c.Abort()
263: 			return
264: 		}
265: 
266: 		// TODO: Implement proper TokenReview validation
267: 		c.Next()
268: 	}
269: }
270: 
271: func startGarbageCollector(storage store.Store, dynamicClient dynamic.Interface, config *rest.Config) {
272: 	ticker := time.NewTicker(15 * time.Second)
273: 	defer ticker.Stop()
274: 
275: 	for range ticker.C {
276: 		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Minute)
277: 		defer cancel()
278: 
279: 		now := time.Now()
280: 		inactiveTime := now.Add(-DefaultSandboxIdleTimeout)
281: 
282: 		// List expired sessions
283: 		expired, err := storage.ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx, now, 16)
284: 		if err != nil {
285: 			klog.Errorf("Failed to list expired sandboxes: %v", err)
286: 			continue
287: 		}
288: 
289: 		// List inactive sessions
290: 		inactive, err := storage.ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx, inactiveTime, 16)
291: 		if err != nil {
292: 			klog.Errorf("Failed to list inactive sandboxes: %v", err)
293: 			continue
294: 		}
295: 
296: 		// Merge and deduplicate
297: 		allSessionIDs := make(map[string]bool)
298: 		for _, id := range expired {
299: 			allSessionIDs[id] = true
300: 		}
301: 		for _, id := range inactive {
302: 			allSessionIDs[id] = true
303: 		}
304: 
305: 		// Delete each sandbox
306: 		for sessionID := range allSessionIDs {
307: 			sandbox, err := storage.GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID)
308: 			if err == store.ErrNotFound {
309: 				continue
310: 			}
311: 			if err != nil {
312: 				klog.Errorf("Failed to get sandbox %s: %v", sessionID, err)
313: 				continue
314: 			}
315: 
316: 			// TODO: Delete actual Kubernetes resources
317: 
318: 			if err := storage.DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID); err != nil {
319: 				klog.Errorf("Failed to delete sandbox %s: %v", sessionID, err)
320: 			}
321: 		}
322: 	}
323: }
324: 
325: func generateSessionID() string {
326: 	return uuid.New().String()
327: }

(End of file - total 327 lines)
</content>

76: 	message := err.Error()
77: 
78: 	if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
79: 		status = http.StatusNotFound
80: 	} else if apierrors.IsBadRequest(err) {
81: 		status = http.StatusBadRequest
82: 	} else if apierrors.IsUnauthorized(err) {
83: 		status = http.StatusUnauthorized
84: 	} else if apierrors.IsForbidden(err) {
85: 		status = http.StatusForbidden
86: 	} else if apierrors.IsConflict(err) {
87: 		status = http.StatusConflict
88: 	} else if apierrors.IsTimeout(err) {
89: 		status = http.StatusRequestTimeout
90: 	} else if apierrors.IsServiceUnavailable(err) {
91: 		status = http.StatusServiceUnavailable
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	// Extract message if available
95: 	if statusErr, ok := err.(apierrors.APIStatus); ok {
96: 		if code := statusErr.Status().Code; code != 0 {
97: 			status = int(code)
98: 		}
99: 		if statusErr.Status().Message != "" {
100: 			message = statusErr.Status().Message
101: 		}
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
105: 	w.WriteHeader(status)
106: 	w.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"error":"%s"}`, message)))
107: }
108: 
109: // CreateSandboxRequest represents a request to create a sandbox
110: type CreateSandboxRequest struct {
111: 	Kind      string                 `json:"kind" binding:"required"`
112: 	Name      string                 `json:"name" binding:"required"`
113: 	Namespace string                 `json:"namespace" binding:"required"`
114: 	Metadata  map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
115: }
116: 
117: // Validate validates the create sandbox request
118: func (r *CreateSandboxRequest) Validate() error {
119: 	if r.Kind != "AgentRuntime" && r.Kind != "CodeInterpreter" {
120: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid kind: %s, must be AgentRuntime or CodeInterpreter", r.Kind)
121: 	}
122: 	if r.Namespace == "" {
123: 		return errors.New("namespace is required")
124: 	}
125: 	if r.Name == "" {
126: 		return errors.New("name is required")
127: 	}
128: 	return nil
129: }
130: 
131: // CreateSandboxResponse represents a response for sandbox creation
132: type CreateSandboxResponse struct {
133: 	SessionID   string       `json:"sessionId"`
134: 	SandboxID   string       `json:"sandboxId"`
135: 	SandboxName string       `json:"sandboxName"`
136: 	EntryPoints []EntryPoint `json:"entryPoints"`
137: }
138: 
139: // EntryPoint represents an endpoint for accessing a sandbox
140: type EntryPoint struct {
141: 	Path     string `json:"path"`
142: 	Protocol string `json:"protocol"`
143: 	Endpoint string `json:"endpoint"`
144: }
145: 
146: // SandboxStatusResponse represents sandbox status information
147: type SandboxStatusResponse struct {
148: 	SessionID     string             `json:"sessionId"`
149: 	SandboxID     string             `json:"sandboxId"`
150: 	SandboxName   string             `json:"sandboxName"`
151: 	Namespace     string             `json:"namespace"`
152: 	Kind          string             `json:"kind"`
153: 	Status        string             `json:"status"`
154: 	Conditions    []metav1.Condition `json:"conditions,omitempty"`
155: 	EntryPoints   []EntryPoint       `json:"entryPoints,omitempty"`
156: 	AgentEndpoint string             `json:"agentEndpoint,omitempty"`
157: }

(End of file - total 157 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Integrations Specification
2: 
3: ## Purpose
4: 
5: Normative requirements for the AgentCube Dify plugin integration and the PCAP analyzer example that uses AgentCube code interpreters and Kubernetes sandboxes.
6: 
7: ## Requirements
8: 
9: ### Requirement: Dify plugin bootstrap and runtime
10: 
11: The system SHALL expose a Dify-compatible Python plugin whose process entry runs the framework runner with a configured maximum request timeout.
12: 
13: #### Scenario: Plugin process starts
14: 
15: - **GIVEN** the plugin package is installed with compatible `dify-plugin` and `agentcube-sdk` versions
16: - **WHEN** the platform starts the plugin using the manifest-declared entrypoint
17: - **THEN** the plugin SHALL execute `main` which runs `Plugin(DifyPluginEnv(MAX_REQUEST_TIMEOUT=120)).run()` until the host stops it
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ### Requirement: Plugin manifest structure
22: 
23: The system SHALL ship a root `manifest.yaml` declaring plugin metadata, tool provider registration, resource permissions, and runner metadata sufficient for Dify to load and execute the plugin.
24: 
25: #### Scenario: Required manifest fields
26: 
27: - **GIVEN** a valid plugin package root
28: - **WHEN** the manifest is parsed by a Dify-compatible toolchain
29: - **THEN** the manifest SHALL include `version`, `type: plugin`, `author`, `name`, `label` (multilingual map), `description` (multilingual), `icon`, `icon_dark`, `resource` (including `memory` and `permission` subtree), `plugins.tools` listing provider descriptors, and `meta` containing `version`, `arch`, `runner` (`language`, `version`, `entrypoint`), and `minimum_dify_version`
30: 
31: #### Scenario: Tool provider wiring
32: 
33: - **GIVEN** the manifest `plugins.tools` references the Agentcube provider descriptor
34: - **WHEN** the plugin is loaded
35: - **THEN** Dify SHALL resolve `provider/agentcube.yaml` and associate it with the Python module declared under `extra.python.source`
36: 
37: ---
38: 
39: ### Requirement: Provider descriptor structure
40: 
41: The system SHALL define a tool provider YAML that identifies the provider, lists its tools, and points to the Python provider implementation.
42: 
43: #### Scenario: Provider lists tools and entry module
44: 
45: - **GIVEN** `provider/agentcube.yaml` is present
46: - **WHEN** the integration runtime resolves the provider
47: - **THEN** the descriptor SHALL include `identity` (author, name, multilingual label/description, icon), a `tools` list including `tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.yaml`, and `extra.python.source: provider/agentcube.py`
48: 
49: ---
50: 
51: ### Requirement: Tool parameter schema
52: 
53: The system SHALL declare a tool schema for the code interpreter integration including connection endpoints, optional language selection, and code/command/session fields.
54: 
55: #### Scenario: Declared parameters match remote APIs
56: 
57: - **GIVEN** `tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.yaml`
58: - **WHEN** an operator configures the tool in Dify
59: - **THEN** the schema SHALL require string parameters `router_url` and `workload_manager_url`, SHALL offer optional `language` as a select among `python`, `javascript`, and `typescript`, and SHALL allow optional `code`, `command`, `session_id`, `session_reuse` (boolean), and `code_interpreter_id`, each documented for human and LLM-oriented forms
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ### Requirement: Tool provider credential validation
64: 
65: The system SHALL implement credential validation on the tool provider class and SHALL surface validation failures using the Dify tool provider error type.
66: 
67: #### Scenario: Invalid credentials are rejected
68: 
69: - **GIVEN** an instance of `AgentcubeCodeInterpreterProvider`
70: - **WHEN** `_validate_credentials` encounters an error condition the implementer treats as invalid
71: - **THEN** the provider SHALL raise `ToolProviderCredentialValidationError` with a string message derived from the failure
72: 
73: #### Scenario: Successful validation completes quietly
74: 
75: - **GIVEN** credentials that satisfy the provider’s validation rules
76: - **WHEN** `_validate_credentials` runs
77: - **THEN** the method SHALL return without raising
78: 
79: ---
80: 
81: ### Requirement: Tool invocation and JSON messaging
82: 
83: The system SHALL implement the Agentcube code interpreter tool as a Dify `Tool` that delegates execution and returns structured JSON to the host.
84: 
85: #### Scenario: Dify invokes the tool
86: 
87: - **GIVEN** a configured tool invocation with parameters matching the schema
88: - **WHEN** Dify calls `_invoke` on `AgentcubeCodeInterpreterTool`
89: - **THEN** the tool SHALL call `execute(**tool_parameters)` and SHALL yield `create_json_message(result)` for the outcome
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ### Requirement: Tool execution API surface
94: 
95: The system SHALL implement `execute` with parameters for router and workload manager URLs, optional language, session controls, and code or command execution, delegating to `agentcube.CodeInterpreterClient`.
96: 
97: #### Scenario: Code or command runs against configured endpoints
98: 
99: - **GIVEN** valid `router_url`, `workload_manager_url`, and optional `language` defaulting to `python`
100: - **WHEN** the tool receives `code` and/or `command` with optional `session_id`, `session_reuse`, and `code_interpreter_id`
101: - **THEN** the tool SHALL use the AgentCube Python SDK client to perform the corresponding remote operations and SHALL return a JSON-serializable result object suitable for `create_json_message`
102: 
103: ---
104: 
105: ### Requirement: Plugin Python dependencies
106: 
107: The system SHALL pin compatible versions of `dify-plugin` and `agentcube-sdk` for reproducible installs.
108: 
109: #### Scenario: Dependency bounds
110: 
111: - **GIVEN** `integrations/dify-plugin/requirements.txt`
112: - **WHEN** dependencies are installed in a clean environment
113: - **THEN** installs SHALL satisfy `dify-plugin>=0.4.2,<0.5.0` and `agentcube-sdk>=0.0.10`
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: ### Requirement: PCAP analyzer FastAPI service
118: 
119: The system SHALL provide a FastAPI application that validates OpenAI-compatible configuration at startup, composes LangChain/LangGraph agents, and exposes an analysis endpoint for PCAP uploads.
120: 
121: #### Scenario: Startup requires API key
122: 
123: - **GIVEN** the process is started without a usable `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or equivalent configured API key)
124: - **WHEN** the application completes startup hooks
125: - **THEN** startup SHALL fail rather than serve requests without credentials
126: 
127: #### Scenario: Analyze endpoint accepts PCAP input
128: 
129: - **GIVEN** a running service with valid LLM configuration
130: - **WHEN** a client calls `POST /analyze` with either an uploaded PCAP file or a `pcap_path` form field
131: - **THEN** the handler SHALL return a body matching `AnalyzeResponse` including `script`, `results`, and `report`
132: 
133: ---
134: 
135: ### Requirement: PCAP analyzer configuration surface
136: 
137: The system SHALL read runtime configuration from environment variables for the OpenAI-compatible API, model selection, and AgentCube sandbox parameters.
138: 
139: #### Scenario: Defaults and overrides
140: 
141: - **GIVEN** standard deployment environment variables
142: - **WHEN** the service starts
143: - **THEN** it SHALL use `OPENAI_API_BASE` defaulting to `https://api.siliconflow.cn/v1`, `OPENAI_MODEL` defaulting to `Qwen/QwQ-32B`, `CODEINTERPRETER_NAME` defaulting to `my-interpreter`, `SANDBOX_NAMESPACE` defaulting to `default`, and `SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC` defaulting to `5`, while binding HTTP to `0.0.0.0:8000` as defined in the application
144: 
145: ---
146: 
147: ### Requirement: PCAP analyzer sandbox integration
148: 
149: The system SHALL orchestrate PCAP analysis by planning and repairing scripts, executing them inside an AgentCube-backed sandbox via `SandboxRunner`, and aggregating results through reporter agents.
150: 
151: #### Scenario: Sandbox runner executes commands
152: 
153: - **GIVEN** a `SandboxRunner` constructed with interpreter name, namespace, and warmup duration
154: - **WHEN** analysis logic invokes `run` with a shell command
155: - **THEN** the runner SHALL execute the command in the remote sandbox context and SHALL return structured execution results for downstream LLM steps
156: 
157: #### Scenario: File staging uses runner uploads
158: 
159: - **GIVEN** a local PCAP path or bytes to analyze
160: - **WHEN** the orchestration needs the artifact inside the sandbox workspace
161: - **THEN** the system SHALL use `upload_file` or `upload_bytes` on `SandboxRunner` before executing analysis commands
162: 
163: ---
164: 
165: ### Requirement: PCAP analyzer container image
166: 
167: The system SHALL build a container image that installs Python dependencies with `uv`, vendors the in-repo `agentcube` SDK copy, sets `PYTHONPATH`, exposes port 8000, and starts the FastAPI app via `uv run`.
168: 
169: #### Scenario: Image entrypoint
170: 
171: - **GIVEN** the `example/pcap-analyzer/Dockerfile` build context
172: - **WHEN** the container runs without overriding the command
173: - **THEN** the default command SHALL be `uv run pcap_analyzer.py` with `PYTHONPATH="/app"` and port `8000` exposed
174: 
175: ---
176: 
177: ### Requirement: PCAP analyzer Kubernetes deployment
178: 
179: The system SHALL supply a `Deployment` manifest that runs the analyzer image with resource requests/limits and environment variables wired from a Secret and plain env for AgentCube and model endpoints.
180: 
181: #### Scenario: Deployment wiring
182: 
183: - **GIVEN** `example/pcap-analyzer/deployment.yaml` applied to the cluster
184: - **WHEN** the workload becomes ready
185: - **THEN** the pod SHALL run image `pcap-analyzer:latest` (pull `IfNotPresent`), listen on TCP 8000, request `cpu: 200m` / `memory: 100Mi` and limit `cpu: 1` / `memory: 1Gi`, mount `OPENAI_API_KEY` from Secret `pcap-analyzer-secrets` key `openai-api-key`, and set `OPENAI_API_BASE`, `OPENAI_MODEL`, `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL`, `ROUTER_URL`, `CODEINTERPRETER_NAME`, `SANDBOX_NAMESPACE`, and `SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC` as container environment variables
186: 
187: #### Scenario: Process launch in cluster
188: 
189: - **GIVEN** the deployment’s container specification
190: - **WHEN** Kubernetes starts the container
191: - **THEN** the command SHALL be `uv` with arguments `run pcap_analyzer.py`, matching local Docker behavior

(End of file - total 191 lines)
</content>

<content>1: # Code Execution (PicoD) — Design (from source)
2: 
3: Derived from `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/picod` and `cmd/picod` in `/tmp/agentcube-ref`. Field names, types, tags, and constants match the Go definitions.
4: 
5: ## Packages and entrypoint
6: 
7: - **Main**: `cmd/picod/main.go` — parses flags, constructs `picod.Config`, `picod.NewServer(config)`, `server.Run()`.
8: 
9: ## CLI flags and defaults
10: 
11: | Flag        | Type   | Default | Usage string |
12: |------------|--------|---------|--------------|
13: | `-port`    | `int`  | `8080`  | `Port for the PicoD server to listen on` |
14: | `-workspace` | `string` | `""` | `Root directory for file operations (default: current working directory)` |
15: 
16: `klog.InitFlags(nil)` is called so standard klog flags are also registered.
17: 
18: ## Environment variables
19: 
20: | Name | Constant | Usage |
21: |------|----------|--------|
22: | `PICOD_AUTH_PUBLIC_KEY` | `PublicKeyEnvVar` | PEM-encoded PKIX public key (`PUBLIC KEY` block). Loaded in `NewServer` via `AuthManager.LoadPublicKeyFromEnv()`. If empty, invalid PEM, or not `*rsa.PublicKey`, `LoadPublicKeyFromEnv` returns an error and `NewServer` calls `klog.Fatalf` — **no serve without a valid key**. |
23: 
24: ## Server configuration struct
25: 
26: ```go
27: type Config struct {
28: 	Port      int    `json:"port"`
29: 	Workspace string `json:"workspace"`
30: }
31: ```
32: 
33: ## Internal server fields (`Server`)
34: 
35: - `engine *gin.Engine`
36: - `config Config`
37: - `authManager *AuthManager`
38: - `startTime time.Time` — set to `time.Now()` in `NewServer`
39: - `workspaceDir string` — absolute workspace root after `setWorkspace`
40: 
41: ### Workspace resolution
42: 
43: - If `config.Workspace != ""`: `setWorkspace(config.Workspace)` → `filepath.Abs` when possible, else fallback to provided string.
44: - Else: `os.Getwd()` (fatal on error).
45: 
46: ## Constants
47: 
48: | Name | Value | File | Meaning |
49: |------|-------|------|---------|
50: | `TimeoutExitCode` | `124` | `execute.go` | Exit code when context deadline exceeded |
51: | `MaxBodySize` | `32 << 20` (32 MiB) | `auth.go` | `http.MaxBytesReader` cap applied after successful JWT check |
52: | `PublicKeyEnvVar` | `"PICOD_AUTH_PUBLIC_KEY"` | `auth.go` | Env var for PEM public key |
53: | `maxFileMode` | `0777` | `files.go` | `parseFileMode` rejects modes `> 0777` (falls back to `0644`) |
54: 
55: ### Default timeouts and modes
56: 
57: - **Execute default timeout** (when `ExecuteRequest.Timeout == ""`): `60 * time.Second` (note: struct comment in source says `"30s"` but code uses 60s).
58: - **Directory creation** (`MkdirAll`): `0755`
59: - **Default file mode** (`parseFileMode` empty/invalid/too large): `0644` (octal parsed with `strconv.ParseUint(modeStr, 8, 32)`)
60: 
61: ## Request / response structs (exact JSON tags)
62: 
63: ### Execute
64: 
65: ```go
66: type ExecuteRequest struct {
67: 	Command    []string          `json:"command" binding:"required"`
68: 	Timeout    string            `json:"timeout"`
69: 	WorkingDir string            `json:"working_dir"`
70: 	Env        map[string]string `json:"env"`
71: }
72: 
73: type ExecuteResponse struct {
74: 	Stdout    string    `json:"stdout"`
75: 	Stderr    string    `json:"stderr"`
76: 	ExitCode  int       `json:"exit_code"`
77: 	Duration  float64   `json:"duration"`
78: 	StartTime time.Time `json:"start_time"`
79: 	EndTime   time.Time `json:"end_time"`
80: }
81: ```
82: 
83: - **Binding**: `c.ShouldBindJSON(&req)` on `POST /api/execute`.
84: - **Command**: `exec.CommandContext(ctx, req.Command[0], req.Command[1:]...)`
85: - **Stdout/Stderr**: `bytes.Buffer` assigned to `cmd.Stdout` and `cmd.Stderr`; no `cmd.Stdin` assignment.
86: - **Exit code**: If `errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.DeadlineExceeded)` → `TimeoutExitCode`; else if `cmd.ProcessState != nil` → `ExitCode()`; else `1` and append `err.Error()` to stderr (with newline if stderr non-empty).
87: 
88: ### Files — upload response / JSON body
89: 
90: ```go
91: type FileInfo struct {
92: 	Path     string    `json:"path"`
93: 	Size     int64     `json:"size"`
94: 	Mode     string    `json:"mode"`
95: 	Modified time.Time `json:"modified"`
96: }
97: 
98: type UploadFileRequest struct {
99: 	Path    string `json:"path" binding:"required"`
100: 	Content string `json:"content" binding:"required"`
101: 	Mode    string `json:"mode"`
102: }
103: ```
104: 
105: - **Multipart**: `Content-Type` prefix `multipart/form-data` → `handleMultipartUpload`.
106:   - Form: `c.PostForm("path")`, `c.FormFile("file")`, optional `c.PostForm("mode")`.
107: - **JSON**: `c.ShouldBindJSON` → Base64 decode `req.Content` with `base64.StdEncoding`.
108: 
109: ### Files — listing
110: 
111: ```go
112: type FileEntry struct {
113: 	Name     string    `json:"name"`
114: 	Size     int64     `json:"size"`
115: 	Modified time.Time `json:"modified"`
116: 	Mode     string    `json:"mode"`
117: 	IsDir    bool      `json:"is_dir"`
118: }
119: 
120: type ListFilesResponse struct {
121: 	Files []FileEntry `json:"files"`
122: }
123: ```
124: 
125: - **Query**: `path := c.Query("path")` (required non-empty).
126: 
127: ## HTTP error JSON shapes (Gin `gin.H`)
128: 
129: - **Generic** (400/500): `{"error": string, "code": int}` (`code` is numeric HTTP status).
130: - **Auth** (401): `{"error": string, "code": int, "detail": string}`.
131: 
132: ## Gin setup and route registration
133: 
134: Pattern from `NewServer`:
135: 
136: 1. `gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)`
137: 2. `engine := gin.New()`
138: 3. Global: `engine.Use(gin.Logger(), gin.Recovery())`
139: 4. `authManager.LoadPublicKeyFromEnv()` — fatal on error
140: 5. API group:
141: 
142: ```go
143: api := engine.Group("/api")
144: api.Use(s.authManager.AuthMiddleware())
145: {
146: 	api.POST("/execute", s.ExecuteHandler)
147: 	api.POST("/files", s.UploadFileHandler)
148: 	api.GET("/files", s.ListFilesHandler)
149: 	api.GET("/files/*path", s.DownloadFileHandler)
150: }
151: engine.GET("/health", s.HealthCheckHandler)
152: ```
153: 
154: - **Download param**: `path := c.Param("path")`; then `strings.TrimPrefix(path, "/")` before `sanitizePath`.
155: 
156: ## Health response
157: 
158: ```go
159: c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
160: 	"status":  "ok",
161: 	"service": "PicoD",
162: 	"version": "0.0.1",
163: 	"uptime":  time.Since(s.startTime).String(),
164: })
165: ```
166: 
167: ## HTTP server
168: 
169: ```go
170: server := &http.Server{
171: 	Addr:              fmt.Sprintf(":%d", s.config.Port),
172: 	Handler:           s.engine,
173: 	ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second,
174: }
175: return server.ListenAndServe()
176: ```
177: 
178: ## JWT middleware (`AuthManager`)
179: 
180: ### Types
181: 
182: ```go
183: type AuthManager struct {
184: 	publicKey *rsa.PublicKey
185: 	mutex     sync.RWMutex
186: }
187: ```
188: 
189: ### Key loading (`LoadPublicKeyFromEnv`)
190: 
191: 1. `keyData := os.Getenv(PublicKeyEnvVar)`
192: 2. If empty → error: `environment variable %s is not set`
193: 3. `pem.Decode([]byte(keyData))` — nil block → error `failed to decode PEM block`
194: 4. `x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey(block.Bytes)` — on success assert `*rsa.PublicKey`
195: 
196: ### `AuthMiddleware` behavior
197: 
198: 1. `authHeader := c.GetHeader("Authorization")`
199: 2. If empty → 401 JSON missing header / JWT required detail
200: 3. `parts := strings.Split(authHeader, " ")` — require `len(parts) == 2` and `parts[0] == "Bearer"` else 401 invalid format (`Use Bearer <token>`)
201: 4. `tokenString := parts[1]`
202: 5. `jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) { ... }, jwt.WithExpirationRequired(), jwt.WithIssuedAt(), jwt.WithLeeway(time.Minute))`
203:    - Key func: require `token.Method` is `*jwt.SigningMethodRSA`; return `am.publicKey` (read lock)
204: 6. If `err != nil || !token.Valid` → 401 `Invalid token` + detail with verification error
205: 7. `c.Request.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(c.Writer, c.Request.Body, MaxBodySize)`
206: 8. `c.Next()`
207: 
208: **Note**: Any RSA signing method instance satisfies the type assert (e.g. RS256 in tests); HS256 is rejected.
209: 
210: ## Path security (`sanitizePath`)
211: 
212: Used by upload, list, download, and execute `working_dir`.
213: 
214: 1. If `s.workspaceDir == ""` → error `workspace directory not initialized`
215: 2. `resolvedWorkspace := filepath.EvalSymlinks(s.workspaceDir)`; on error fall back to `filepath.Abs` or `filepath.Clean`
216: 3. `resolvedWorkspace = filepath.Clean(resolvedWorkspace)`
217: 4. `cleanPath := filepath.Clean(p)`; if absolute, strip leading `os.PathSeparator` from path string
218: 5. `fullPathCandidate := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(resolvedWorkspace, cleanPath))`
219: 6. `relPath, relErr := filepath.Rel(resolvedWorkspace, fullPathCandidate)` — error → access denied
220: 7. If `strings.HasPrefix(relPath, ".."+separator)` or `relPath == ".."` → access denied
221: 8. `resolvedFinalPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(fullPathCandidate)`:
222:    - If success: re-check `filepath.Rel` against `resolvedWorkspace` for `..` escape; pass → return `resolvedFinalPath`
223:    - If error (e.g. path does not exist): return `fullPathCandidate` (already verified as inside workspace)
224: 
225: **Upload/list responses** use `filepath.Rel(s.workspaceDir, safePath)` for JSON `path` on uploaded files (not `resolvedWorkspace` symlink expansion — `safePath` is the sanitized target path).
226: 
227: ## Multipart vs JSON detection
228: 
229: ```go
230: contentType := c.ContentType()
231: if strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "multipart/form-data") {
232: 	s.handleMultipartUpload(c)
233: } else {
234: 	s.handleJSONBase64Upload(c)
235: }
236: ```
237: 
238: ## File download headers (exact strings)
239: 
240: - `Content-Description`: `File Transfer`
241: - `Content-Transfer-Encoding`: `binary`
242: - `Content-Disposition`: `fmt.Sprintf("attachment; filename=\"%s\"", filepath.Base(safePath))`
243: - `Content-Type`: `mime.TypeByExtension(filepath.Ext(safePath))` or `"application/octet-stream"`
244: 
245: This document is sufficient to reconstruct handlers, routes, structs, and security checks without reading the repo.

(End of file - total 245 lines)
</content>

<content>1: # Sandbox Orchestration — Design
2: 
3: ## Module and dependencies
4: 
5: | Item | Value |
6: |------|--------|
7: | Go module | `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube` |
8: | Go version (go.mod) | `1.24.4` |
9: | Toolchain | `go1.24.9` |
10: 
11: **Direct `require` (from go.mod):**  
12: `github.com/agiledragon/gomonkey/v2 v2.13.0`, `github.com/alicebob/miniredis/v2 v2.35.0`, `github.com/gin-gonic/gin v1.10.0`, `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5 v5.2.2`, `github.com/google/uuid v1.6.0`, `github.com/redis/go-redis/v9 v9.17.1`, `github.com/stretchr/testify v1.11.1`, `github.com/valkey-io/valkey-go v1.0.69`, `golang.org/x/net v0.47.0`, `k8s.io/api v0.34.1`, `k8s.io/apimachinery v0.34.1`, `k8s.io/client-go v0.34.1`, `k8s.io/klog/v2 v2.130.1`, `k8s.io/utils v0.0.0-20251002143259-bc988d571ff4`, `sigs.k8s.io/agent-sandbox v0.1.1`, `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime v0.22.2`
13: 
14: ## Package `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/apis/runtime/v1alpha1`
15: 
16: ### groupversion_info.go — package and scheme
17: 
18: ```go
19: // +groupName=runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh
20: package v1alpha1
21: 
22: var (
23:     GroupVersion = schema.GroupVersion{Group: "runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh", Version: "v1alpha1"}
24:     SchemeBuilder = &scheme.Builder{GroupVersion: GroupVersion}
25:     AddToScheme = SchemeBuilder.AddToScheme
26: )
27: ```
28: 
29: ### register.go — kind metadata
30: 
31: ```go
32: var (
33:     CodeInterpreterKind             = "CodeInterpreter"
34:     CodeInterpreterGroupKind        = GroupVersion.WithKind("CodeInterpreter")
35:     CodeInterpreterListKind         = "CodeInterpreterList"
36:     CodeInterpreterGroupVersionKind = GroupVersion.WithKind("CodeInterpreter")
37: )
38: 
39: var (
40:     AgentRuntimeKind             = "AgentRuntime"
41:     AgentRuntimeListKind         = "AgentRuntimeList"
42:     AgentRuntimeGroupVersionKind = GroupVersion.WithKind("AgentRuntime")
43: )
44: 
45: var SchemeGroupVersion = GroupVersion
46: 
47: func Resource(resource string) schema.GroupVersionResource {
48:     return GroupVersion.WithResource(resource)
49: }
50: ```
51: 
52: ### agent_type.go — AgentRuntime types
53: 
54: ```go
55: // +genclient
56: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
57: // +kubebuilder:object:root=true
58: // +kubebuilder:subresource:status
59: // +kubebuilder:resource:scope=Namespaced
60: // +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Age",type="date",JSONPath=".metadata.creationTimestamp"
61: type AgentRuntime struct {
62:     metav1.TypeMeta   `json:",inline"`
63:     metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
64:     Spec   AgentRuntimeSpec   `json:"spec"`
65:     Status AgentRuntimeStatus `json:"status,omitempty"`
66: }
67: 
68: type AgentRuntimeSpec struct {
69:     Ports []TargetPort `json:"targetPort"`
70:     // +kubebuilder:validation:Required
71:     Template *SandboxTemplate `json:"podTemplate" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=podTemplate"`
72:     // +kubebuilder:validation:Required
73:     // +kubebuilder:default="15m"
74:     SessionTimeout *metav1.Duration `json:"sessionTimeout,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,opt,name=sessionTimeout"`
75:     // +kubebuilder:validation:Required
76:     // +kubebuilder:default="8h"
77:     MaxSessionDuration *metav1.Duration `json:"maxSessionDuration,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,3,opt,name=maxSessionDuration"`
78: }
79: 
80: type AgentRuntimeStatus struct {

84: 
85: type SandboxTemplate struct {

90:     // +kubebuilder:validation:Required
91:     Spec corev1.PodSpec `json:"spec" protobuf:"bytes,3,opt,name=spec"`

93: 
94: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
95: // +kubebuilder:object:root=true
96: type AgentRuntimeList struct {
97:     metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`

100: }
101: ```
102: 
103: ### codeinterpreter_types.go — CodeInterpreter and shared types
104: 
105: ```go
106: // +genclient
107: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
108: // +kubebuilder:object:root=true
109: // +kubebuilder:subresource:status
110: // +kubebuilder:resource:scope=Namespaced
111: // +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Age",type="date",JSONPath=".metadata.creationTimestamp"
112: type CodeInterpreter struct {
113:     metav1.TypeMeta   `json:",inline"`
114:     metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`

142: 
143: type CodeInterpreterSandboxTemplate struct {
144:     // +optional

146:     // +optional
147:     Annotations map[string]string `json:"annotations,omitempty"`

204: ## Package `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/common/types`
205: 
206: ```go
207: const (
208:     AgentRuntimeKind    = "AgentRuntime"
209:     CodeInterpreterKind = "CodeInterpreter"
210:     SandboxKind         = "Sandbox"
211:     SandboxClaimsKind   = "SandboxClaim"
212: )

214: type SandboxInfo struct {

238: type CreateSandboxResponse struct {
239:     SessionID   string              `json:"sessionId"`

241:     SandboxName string              `json:"sandboxName"`
242:     EntryPoints []SandboxEntryPoint `json:"entryPoints"`
243: }
244: 
245: func (car *CreateSandboxRequest) Validate() error
246: ```
247: 
248: ## Package `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/api`
249: 
250: ```go
251: const (
252:     resourceGroup               = "agentcube.volcano.sh"
253:     sessionResourceName         = "sessions"
254:     agentRuntimeResourceName    = "agentruntimes"
255:     codeInterpreterResourceName = "codeinterpreters"
256: )
257: 
258: var (
259:     ErrAgentRuntimeNotFound    = errors.New("agent runtime not found")
260:     ErrCodeInterpreterNotFound = errors.New("code interpreter not found")
261:     ErrTemplateMissing         = errors.New("resource has no pod template")
262:     ErrPublicKeyMissing        = errors.New("public key not yet loaded from Router Secret")
263: )
264: 
265: func NewSessionNotFoundError(sessionID string) error
266: func NewSandboxTemplateNotFoundError(namespace, name, kind string) error
267: func NewUpstreamUnavailableError(err error) error
268: func NewInternalError(err error) error
269: ```
270: 
271: ## Package `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/workloadmanager`
272: 
273: ### Config and Server
274: 
275: ```go
276: type Config struct {
277:     Port             string
278:     RuntimeClassName string
279:     EnableTLS        bool
280:     TLSCert          string
281:     TLSKey           string
282:     EnableAuth       bool
283: }
284: 
285: type Server struct {
286:     config            *Config
287:     router            *gin.Engine
288:     httpServer        *http.Server
289:     k8sClient         *K8sClient
290:     sandboxController *SandboxReconciler
291:     tokenCache        *TokenCache
292:     informers         *Informers
293:     storeClient       store.Store
294:     wg                sync.WaitGroup
295: }
296: ```
297: 
298: ### HTTP routes
299: 
300: | Method | Path | Auth middleware | Handler |
301: |--------|------|-----------------|---------|
302: | GET | `/health` | No | `handleHealth` |
303: | POST | `/v1/agent-runtime` | If `EnableAuth` | `handleAgentRuntimeCreate` |
304: | DELETE | `/v1/agent-runtime/sessions/:sessionId` | If `EnableAuth` | `handleDeleteSandbox` |
305: | POST | `/v1/code-interpreter` | If `EnableAuth` | `handleCodeInterpreterCreate` |
306: | DELETE | `/v1/code-interpreter/sessions/:sessionId` | If `EnableAuth` | `handleDeleteSandbox` |
307: 
308: ### ErrorResponse
309: 
310: ```go
311: type ErrorResponse struct {
312:     Message string `json:"message"`
313: }
314: ```
315: 
316: ### K8sClient and defaults
317: 
318: ```go
319: const (
320:     DefaultSandboxTTL         = 8 * time.Hour
321:     DefaultSandboxIdleTimeout = 15 * time.Minute
322: )
323: 
324: var (
325:     SessionIdLabelKey         = "runtime.agentcube.io/session-id"
326:     WorkloadNameLabelKey      = "runtime.agentcube.io/workload-name"
327:     SandboxNameLabelKey       = "runtime.agentcube.io/sandbox-name"
328:     LastActivityAnnotationKey = "last-activity-time"
329:     IdleTimeoutAnnotationKey  = "runtime.agentcube.io/idle-timeout"
330: )
331: 
332: type K8sClient struct {
333:     clientset       *kubernetes.Clientset
334:     dynamicClient   dynamic.Interface
335:     scheme          *runtime.Scheme
336:     baseConfig      *rest.Config
337:     clientCache     *ClientCache
338:     dynamicInformer dynamicinformer.DynamicSharedInformerFactory
339:     informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory
340:     podInformer     cache.SharedIndexInformer
341:     podLister       listersv1.PodLister
342: }
343: ```
344: 
345: - REST config: `QPS = 50`, `Burst = 100`; in-cluster config else kubeconfig loading rules.
346: - `NewClientCache(100)` for user dynamic clients.
347: 
348: ### GVR variables (`informers.go`)
349: 
350: ```go
351: var (
352:     AgentRuntimeGVR = schema.GroupVersionResource{
353:         Group: "runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh", Version: "v1alpha1", Resource: "agentruntimes",
354:     }
355:     CodeInterpreterGVR = schema.GroupVersionResource{
356:         Group: "runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh", Version: "v1alpha1", Resource: "codeinterpreters",
357:     }
358:     SandboxGVR = schema.GroupVersionResource{
359:         Group: "agents.x-k8s.io", Version: "v1alpha1", Resource: "sandboxes",
360:     }
361:     SandboxClaimGVR = schema.GroupVersionResource{
362:         Group: "extensions.agents.x-k8s.io", Version: "v1alpha1", Resource: "sandboxclaims",
363:     }
364: )
365: ```
366: 
367: ### Identity secret (workload_builder.go)
368: 
369: ```go
370: const (
371:     IdentitySecretName = "picod-router-identity"
372:     PublicKeyDataKey   = "public.pem"
373: )
374: 
375: var IdentitySecretNamespace = "default" // overridden by env AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE if set
376: ```
377: 
378: ### Sandbox reconciler
379: 
380: ```go
381: type SandboxReconciler struct {
382:     client.Client
383:     Scheme *runtime.Scheme
384:     watchers map[types.NamespacedName]chan SandboxStatusUpdate
385:     mu       sync.RWMutex
386: }
387: 
388: type SandboxStatusUpdate struct {
389:     Sandbox *sandboxv1alpha1.Sandbox
390: }
391: ```
392: 
393: - `Reconcile`: when `getSandboxStatus(sandbox) == "running"`, notify waiter channel (buffer 1), non-blocking send with `default` branch warning on full channel.
394: - `getSandboxStatus`: returns `"running"` if a condition has `Type == string(sandboxv1alpha1.SandboxConditionReady)` and `Status == metav1.ConditionTrue`, else `"unknown"`.
395: 
396: ### Garbage collector
397: 
398: ```go
399: const gcOnceTimeout = 2 * time.Minute
400: 
401: type garbageCollector struct {
402:     k8sClient   *K8sClient
403:     interval    time.Duration
404:     storeClient store.Store
405: }
406: ```
407: 
408: - `newGarbageCollector(k8s, store, 15*time.Second)` — **GC interval: 15s**.
409: - Per tick: `ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx, time.Now().Add(-DefaultSandboxIdleTimeout), 16)` — **batch size 16**.
410: - `ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx, time.Now(), 16)` — **batch size 16**.
411: - Single tick context timeout: **2 minutes**.
412: 
413: ### Token cache
414: 
415: - `NewTokenCache(1000, 5*time.Minute)` — max entries **1000**, TTL **5m** per entry (LRU + last-access expiry).
416: 
417: ### Auth context keys
418: 
419: ```go
420: type contextKey string
421: 
422: const (
423:     contextKeyUserToken          contextKey = "userToken"
424:     contextKeyServiceAccount     contextKey = "serviceAccount"
425:     contextKeyServiceAccountName contextKey = "serviceAccountName"
426:     contextKeyNamespace          contextKey = "namespace"
427: )
428: ```
429: 
430: ### CLI flags (`cmd/workload-manager/main.go`)
431: 
432: | Flag | Default | Meaning |
433: |------|---------|---------|
434: | `-port` | `8080` | API port |
435: | `-runtime-class-name` | `kuasar-vmm` | Stored on `Config.RuntimeClassName` (flag parsed in `main`; **not read** elsewhere in `pkg/workloadmanager` in current source) |
436: | `-enable-tls` | `false` | TLS |
437: | `-tls-cert` | `""` | Cert path |
438: | `-tls-key` | `""` | Key path |
439: | `-enable-auth` | `false` | TokenReview auth |
440: 
441: - Controller-runtime manager: metrics bind `0`, health probe `0`.
442: - Shutdown: HTTP shutdown timeout **15s**, then cancel ctx, `WaitForBackgroundWorkers`, `CloseStore`.
443: 
444: ### Store dependency
445: 
446: ```go
447: // github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/store
448: type Store interface {
449:     Ping(ctx context.Context) error
450:     GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*types.SandboxInfo, error)
451:     StoreSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandboxStore *types.SandboxInfo) error
452:     UpdateSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandboxStore *types.SandboxInfo) error
453:     DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error
454:     ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int64) ([]*types.SandboxInfo, error)
455:     ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int64) ([]*types.SandboxInfo, error)
456:     UpdateSessionLastActivity(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, at time.Time) error
457:     Close() error
458: }
459: ```
460: 
461: Server uses `store.Storage()` singleton.
462: 
463: ### Sandbox build notes
464: 
465: - AgentRuntime: sandbox name `{workloadName}-{RandString(8)}`, UUID session ID, ports from `spec.Ports`, TTL/idle from `MaxSessionDuration` / `SessionTimeout` when non-nil.
466: - CodeInterpreter: default ports if empty: single `TargetPort{Port:8080, Protocol:ProtocolTypeHTTP, PathPrefix:"/"}`; if `WarmPoolSize > 0`: creates `SandboxClaim` + minimal `Sandbox` metadata only, `sandboxEntry.Kind = SandboxClaimsKind`; else builds full `Sandbox` with container `code-interpreter` and optional `PICOD_AUTH_PUBLIC_KEY` when `AuthMode == picod`.
467: - `buildSandboxObject`: APIVersion `agents.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1`, Kind `Sandbox`, labels include `managed-by: agentcube-workload-manager`, annotation `IdleTimeoutAnnotationKey` = idle duration string, lifecycle `ShutdownTime` = now+TTL, `Replicas: ptr.To[int32](1)`.
468: 
469: ### Pod name annotation
470: 
471: - Read from created sandbox: key from `sigs.k8s.io/agent-sandbox/controllers`.`SandboxPodNameAnnotation` (comment in source documents value `agents.x-k8s.io/sandbox-pod-name`).
472: 
473: ## CodeInterpreter controller (supplementary)
474: 
475: - Reconciles `CodeInterpreter`; when `WarmPoolSize > 0` ensures `SandboxTemplate` (name = CI name) and `SandboxWarmPool` (name = CI name, replicas = WarmPoolSize, templateRef name = CI name); injects `PICOD_AUTH_PUBLIC_KEY` in template unless `AuthModeNone`; requeues 5s if public key not cached and auth not none.

(End of file - total 475 lines)
</content>

```go
// +groupName=runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh
package v1alpha1

15: package store
16: 
17: import (
18: 	"context"
19: 	"errors"
20: 	"fmt"
21: 	"os"
22: 	"strings"
23: 	"sync"
24: 	"time"
25: 
26: 	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
27: )
28: 
29: var (
30: 	// ErrNotFound is returned when a session key is not found
31: 	ErrNotFound = errors.New("store: not found")
32: )
33: 
34: // Store defines the interface for session storage
35: type Store interface {
36: 	// Ping verifies connectivity to the backend
37: 	Ping(ctx context.Context) error
38: 
39: 	// GetSandboxBySessionID retrieves sandbox metadata by session ID
40: 	GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*SandboxInfo, error)
41: 
42: 	// StoreSandbox persists sandbox metadata
43: 	StoreSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandbox *SandboxInfo) error
44: 
45: 	// UpdateSandbox updates existing sandbox metadata
46: 	UpdateSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandbox *SandboxInfo) error
47: 
48: 	// DeleteSandboxBySessionID removes sandbox by session ID
49: 	DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error
50: 
51: 	// ListExpiredSandboxes returns session IDs with expiry before given time
52: 	ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int) ([]string, error)
53: 
54: 	// ListInactiveSandboxes returns session IDs with last activity before given time
55: 	ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int) ([]string, error)
56: 
57: 	// UpdateSessionLastActivity updates the last activity timestamp
58: 	UpdateSessionLastActivity(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, at time.Time) error
59: 
60: 	// Close closes the store connection
61: 	Close() error
62: }
63: 
64: var (
65: 	storage     Store
66: 	storageOnce sync.Once
67: )
68: 
69: // Storage returns the singleton store instance
70: func Storage() Store {
71: 	storageOnce.Do(func() {
72: 		var err error
73: 		storage, err = initStore()
74: 		if err != nil {
75: 			klog.Fatalf("Failed to initialize storage: %v", err)
76: 		}
77: 	})
78: 	return storage
79: }
80: 
81: func initStore() (Store, error) {
82: 	storeType := strings.ToLower(os.Getenv("STORE_TYPE"))
83: 	if storeType == "" {
84: 		storeType = "redis"
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	switch storeType {
88: 	case "redis":
89: 		return NewRedisStore()
90: 	default:
91: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported STORE_TYPE: %s, must be 'redis'", storeType)
92: 	}
93: }

<content>1: // Copyright 2025 Volcano Authors
2: //
3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at
6: //
7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
8: //
9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13: // limitations under the License.
14: 
15: package store
16: 
17: import (
18: 	"context"
19: 	"encoding/json"
20: 	"errors"
21: 	"fmt"
22: 	"os"
23: 	"strings"
24: 	"time"
25: 
26: 	"github.com/redis/go-redis/v9"
27: 	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
28: )
29: 
30: const (
31: 	sessionKeyPrefix = "session:"
32: 	expiryZSETKey    = "session:expiry"
33: 	activityZSETKey  = "session:last_activity"
34: )
35: 
36: // RedisStore implements Store interface using Redis
37: type RedisStore struct {
38: 	client *redis.Client
39: }
40: 
41: // NewRedisStore creates a new Redis store
42: func NewRedisStore() (*RedisStore, error) {
43: 	addr := os.Getenv("REDIS_ADDR")
44: 	if addr == "" {
45: 		return nil, errors.New("REDIS_ADDR environment variable is required")
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	password := os.Getenv("REDIS_PASSWORD")
49: 	passwordRequired := os.Getenv("REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED")
50: 	if strings.ToLower(passwordRequired) != "false" && password == "" {
51: 		return nil, errors.New("REDIS_PASSWORD environment variable is required (set REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED=false to disable)")
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	client := redis.NewClient(&redis.Options{
55: 		Addr:     addr,
56: 		Password: password,
57: 		DB:       0,
58: 	})
59: 
60: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
61: 	defer cancel()
62: 
63: 	if err := client.Ping(ctx).Err(); err != nil {
64: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to connect to Redis: %w", err)
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	klog.Info("Successfully connected to Redis")
68: 	return &RedisStore{client: client}, nil
69: }
70: 
71: // Ping verifies connectivity
72: func (r *RedisStore) Ping(ctx context.Context) error {
73: 	result, err := r.client.Ping(ctx).Result()
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return err
76: 	}
77: 	if result != "PONG" {
78: 		return fmt.Errorf("unexpected ping response: %s", result)
79: 	}
80: 	return nil
81: }
82: 
83: // GetSandboxBySessionID retrieves sandbox metadata
84: func (r *RedisStore) GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*SandboxInfo, error) {
85: 	key := sessionKeyPrefix + sessionID
86: 	data, err := r.client.Get(ctx, key).Bytes()
87: 	if err == redis.Nil {
88: 		return nil, ErrNotFound
89: 	}
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return nil, err
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	var sandbox SandboxInfo
95: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &sandbox); err != nil {
96: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal sandbox data: %w", err)
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	return &sandbox, nil
100: }
101: 
102: // StoreSandbox persists sandbox metadata
103: func (r *RedisStore) StoreSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandbox *SandboxInfo) error {
104: 	if sandbox == nil {
105: 		return errors.New("sandbox cannot be nil")
106: 	}
107: 	if sandbox.ExpiresAt.IsZero() {
108: 		return errors.New("ExpiresAt cannot be zero")
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	data, err := json.Marshal(sandbox)
112: 	if err != nil {
113: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal sandbox data: %w", err)
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	pipe := r.client.Pipeline()
117: 	key := sessionKeyPrefix + sandbox.SessionID
118: 
119: 	// Use SETNX for atomic creation
120: 	pipe.SetNX(ctx, key, data, 0)
121: 
122: 	// Add to expiry index
123: 	pipe.ZAdd(ctx, expiryZSETKey, redis.Z{
124: 		Score:  float64(sandbox.ExpiresAt.Unix()),
125: 		Member: sandbox.SessionID,
126: 	})
127: 
128: 	// Add to activity index
129: 	activityTime := time.Now()
130: 	if !sandbox.LastActivity.IsZero() {
131: 		activityTime = sandbox.LastActivity
132: 	}
133: 	pipe.ZAdd(ctx, activityZSETKey, redis.Z{
134: 		Score:  float64(activityTime.Unix()),
135: 		Member: sandbox.SessionID,
136: 	})
137: 
138: 	// Execute pipeline atomically
139: 	_, err = pipe.Exec(ctx)
140: 	if err != nil {
141: 		return fmt.Errorf("pipeline execution failed: %w", err)
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	return nil
145: }
146: 
147: // UpdateSandbox updates existing sandbox metadata
148: func (r *RedisStore) UpdateSandbox(ctx context.Context, sandbox *SandboxInfo) error {
149: 	if sandbox == nil {
150: 		return errors.New("sandbox cannot be nil")
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	data, err := json.Marshal(sandbox)
154: 	if err != nil {
155: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal sandbox data: %w", err)
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	key := sessionKeyPrefix + sandbox.SessionID
159: 	result, err := r.client.Set(ctx, key, data, 0).Result()
160: 	if err != nil {
161: 		return err
162: 	}
163: 	if result != "OK" {
164: 		return errors.New("key not exists")
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	return nil
168: }
169: 
170: // DeleteSandboxBySessionID removes sandbox by session ID
171: func (r *RedisStore) DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error {
172: 	key := sessionKeyPrefix + sessionID
173: 
174: 	pipe := r.client.Pipeline()
175: 	pipe.Del(ctx, key)
176: 	pipe.ZRem(ctx, expiryZSETKey, sessionID)
177: 	pipe.ZRem(ctx, activityZSETKey, sessionID)
178: 
179: 	_, err := pipe.Exec(ctx)
180: 	return err
181: }
182: 
183: // ListExpiredSandboxes returns session IDs expired before given time
184: func (r *RedisStore) ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int) ([]string, error) {
185: 	result, err := r.client.ZRangeByScore(ctx, expiryZSETKey, &redis.ZRangeBy{
186: 		Min:    "-inf",
187: 		Max:    fmt.Sprintf("%d", before.Unix()),
188: 		Count:  int64(limit),
189: 		Offset: 0,
190: 	}).Result()
191: 	if err != nil {
192: 		return nil, err
193: 	}
194: 	return result, nil
195: }
196: 
197: // ListInactiveSandboxes returns session IDs inactive before given time
198: func (r *RedisStore) ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx context.Context, before time.Time, limit int) ([]string, error) {
199: 	result, err := r.client.ZRangeByScore(ctx, activityZSETKey, &redis.ZRangeBy{
200: 		Min:    "-inf",
201: 		Max:    fmt.Sprintf("%d", before.Unix()),
202: 		Count:  int64(limit),
203: 		Offset: 0,
204: 	}).Result()
205: 	if err != nil {
206: 		return nil, err
207: 	}
208: 	return result, nil
209: }
210: 
211: // UpdateSessionLastActivity updates the last activity timestamp
212: func (r *RedisStore) UpdateSessionLastActivity(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, at time.Time) error {
213: 	if sessionID == "" {
214: 		return errors.New("session ID cannot be empty")
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	if at.IsZero() {
218: 		at = time.Now()
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	key := sessionKeyPrefix + sessionID
222: 	exists, err := r.client.Exists(ctx, key).Result()
223: 	if err != nil {
224: 		return err
225: 	}
226: 	if exists == 0 {
227: 		return ErrNotFound
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	return r.client.ZAdd(ctx, activityZSETKey, redis.Z{
231: 		Score:  float64(at.Unix()),
232: 		Member: sessionID,
233: 	}).Err()
234: }
235: 
236: // Close closes the Redis connection
237: func (r *RedisStore) Close() error {
238: 	return r.client.Close()
239: }

(End of file - total 239 lines)

state.output:
# AgentCube — OpenSpec Project Context

> Source: [github.com/ShijunDeng/agentcube](https://github.com/ShijunDeng/agentcube)
> Generated: 2026-03-21 (from source tree reverse-engineering)

## Purpose

AgentCube is a Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform for AI agents.
It provisions isolated sandbox pods on demand, routes HTTP traffic to them through
session-aware proxies, and manages their lifecycle (creation, expiry, idle cleanup).

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Language (server) | Go 1.24, module `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube` |
| Language (CLI/SDK) | Python ≥3.10 |
| HTTP framework | Gin (Go), Typer (Python CLI), FastAPI (example) |
| Kubernetes | CRDs (`runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1`), controller-runtime, `sigs.k8s.io/agent-sandbox` |
| Storage | Redis (go-redis/v9), Valkey (valkey-go) |
| Auth | JWT RS256 (golang-jwt/v5), K8s TokenReview |
| Packaging | Helm 3, multi-stage Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD |
| Docs | Docusaurus 3.x |

## Architecture

```
Client / SDK / CLI
  → Router (Deployment :8080)
     ↔ Redis/Valkey (session store)
     → WorkloadManager (Service :8080)
        ↔ Redis/Valkey
        → K8s API: CRDs, Sandbox, Pod, Secret, TokenReview
  Sandbox Pods (with optional PicoD daemon)
  Agentd (idle cleanup controller)
  Optional: Volcano vc-agent-scheduler
```

## Conventions

- Go packages under `pkg/`, binaries under `cmd/`
- Python CLI at `cmd/cli/`, SDK at `sdk-python/`
- JSON tags use camelCase; Go structs use PascalCase
- K8s resources use `runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh` API group
- Normative language: SHALL = mandatory, SHOULD = recommended, MAY = optional

part.text:
Based on my exploration, I can see this is the AgentCube project - a Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform. The project currently has a basic workload manager and store implementation, but is missing several key components. Since I cannot find explicit AR-001 through AR-011 references in the specs, I'll implement the core requirements from the spec documents.

Let me start by implementing the missing critical components:

state.output:
<skill_content name="tdd">
# Skill: tdd

# TDD（测试驱动开发）

先写测试。看它失败。写最少代码通过。

**铁律：没有失败的测试，就没有实现代码。**

## 核心知识

### 为什么顺序绝对不能反？（4 个常见异议的反驳）

| 异议 | 真相 |
|------|------|
| "写完再补测试，也能验证" | 测试写在实现后会立即通过——你永远不知道它是否真的在测你要的东西 |
| "我手工测了所有 case" | 手工测试没有记录、不能重跑、下次改动时你会忘了测什么 |
| "删掉 X 小时的工作太浪费" | 沉没成本谬误。留着无法信任的代码才是浪费 |
| "TDD 是教条，务实应该灵活" | TDD 本身就是务实的——它比事后调试快，能防止回归，是真正的捷径 |

**Tests-after 回答"这段代码做了什么"；tests-first 回答"这段代码应该做什么"。两者不等价。**

### Red-Green-Refactor 循环

```
RED  → 写一个会失败的测试
       ↓ 必须亲眼看到它失败（失败原因要对，不是 typo）
GREEN → 写最少代码让测试通过
       ↓ 必须亲眼看到它通过（其他测试也要绿）
REFACTOR → 消除重复、改善命名
       ↓ 保持绿灯，不添加行为
重复 →
```

**关键决策点**：
- RED 阶段测试立即通过？→ 你在测已有行为，修测试
- RED 阶段报错而非失败？→ 修错误直到"正确地失败"
- GREEN 阶段其他测试挂了？→ 立即修，不要继续
- REFACTOR 后测试变红？→ 撤销 refactor，重来

### Bug Fix 模式

Bug 修复和新功能一样，必须先写失败测试。**入口动作：先填诊断胶囊。**

> 诊断胶囊模板 → [refs/bug-diagnosis-capsule.md](../refs/bug-diagnosis-capsule.md)

```bash
# 0. 填诊断胶囊（现象/证据/假设/诊断策略/超时策略/预警策略）
# 1. 写一个复现 bug 的测试（此时必须红）
# 2. 确认测试以"预期的理由"失败
# 3. 修复代码
# 4. 确认测试通过 → 填胶囊第 8 栏（验收）
# 5. 确认无回归
```

**永远不要在没有测试的情况下修 bug。**

## 流程

```
任务开始
  ↓
写一个描述期望行为的测试
  ↓
跑测试 → 失败？→ 继续
              → 通过？→ 你在测已有行为，修测试
  ↓
写最少实现代码
  ↓
跑测试 → 通过？→ 继续
              → 失败？→ 修代码（不改测试）
  ↓
全量测试通过？→ Refactor → 重复
```

## Quick Reference

### 好测试的标准

- **一个行为**：名字里有"and"？拆分
- **名字描述行为**：`rejects empty email` 好于 `test1`
- **测真实代码**：避免只测 mock

### 口令：立即停止的 Red Flags

听到自己说以下任何一句 → 删掉代码，从测试重新开始：

- "应该能过"、"大概没问题"、"先快速实现一下"
- "测试我写完功能再补"、"这个太简单了不用测"
- "我已经手工验证过了"、"精神一致就够了，不用走形式"
- "这个情况不一样，因为……"、"留着参考，写测试时再删"

**所有这些都是理由化（Rationalization）。没有例外。**

## Common Mistakes

| 错误 | 正确做法 |
|------|----------|
| 先写实现，再补测试 | 删掉实现，从失败测试开始 |
| 跳过"看它失败"步骤 | 必须跑测试亲眼看失败 |
| 测试立即通过就继续 | 停下来——测试没有测到真正的行为 |
| 修 bug 时直接改代码 | 先写复现 bug 的失败测试 |
| GREEN 阶段过度实现 | 只写能通过当前测试的最少代码 |
| mock 了所有东西 | 代码耦合度太高，用依赖注入简化 |

## 和其他 skill 的区别

- **vs `debugging`**：TDD 是**预防性**的（写代码前）；debugging 是**修复性**的（bug 出现后）。两者的交叉点：debugging 发现 bug 后，Phase 4 必须先写失败测试再修（此时切换回 TDD 模式）
- **vs `quality-gate`**：TDD 是开发过程中的纪律；quality-gate 是提交前的验收检查

## 下一步

完成功能实现后 → **直接加载 `quality-gate`** 做提交前验收。不要停下来问铲屎官（§17）。

Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.

package picod

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/base64"
	"encoding/json"
	"io"
	"mime/multipart"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"

	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestHealthEndpoint(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: t.TempDir(),
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	req := httptest.NewRequest("GET", "/health", nil)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "ok", response["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "PicoD", response["service"])
	assert.Equal(t, "0.0.1", response["version"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, response["uptime"])
}

func TestExecuteCommandValidRequest(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"echo", "hello world"},
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ExecuteResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "hello world\n", response.Stdout)
	assert.Equal(t, "", response.Stderr)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, response.ExitCode)
	assert.Greater(t, response.Duration, float64(0))
	assert.False(t, response.StartTime.IsZero())
	assert.False(t, response.EndTime.IsZero())
}

func TestExecuteCommandEmptyCommand(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{},
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusBadRequest, w.Code)
	
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "command cannot be empty", response["error"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(400), response["code"])
}

func TestExecuteCommandInvalidTimeout(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"echo", "test"},
		Timeout: "invalid",
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusBadRequest, w.Code)
	
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Contains(t, response["error"], "invalid timeout")
	assert.Equal(t, float64(400), response["code"])
}

func TestExecuteCommandTimeoutExceeded(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"sleep", "10"},
		Timeout: "500ms",
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ExecuteResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, TimeoutExitCode, response.ExitCode)
	assert.Contains(t, response.Stderr, "timed out")
}

func TestExecuteCommandNonZeroExit(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "exit 42"},
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ExecuteResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, 42, response.ExitCode)
}

func TestExecuteCommandWithWorkingDir(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	testDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, "testdir")
	require.NoError(t, os.Mkdir(testDir, 0755))
	
	testFile := filepath.Join(testDir, "test.txt")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte("test content"), 0644))
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command:    []string{"cat", "test.txt"},
		WorkingDir: "testdir",
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ExecuteResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "test content", response.Stdout)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, response.ExitCode)
}

func TestExecuteCommandWithEnv(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "echo $TEST_VAR"},
		Env:     map[string]string{"TEST_VAR": "test_value"},
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ExecuteResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "test_value\n", response.Stdout)
}

func TestExecuteCommandMissingAuth(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := ExecuteRequest{
		Command: []string{"echo", "test"},
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/execute", bytes.NewReader(body))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusUnauthorized, w.Code)
	
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Contains(t, response["error"], "Authorization")
	assert.Equal(t, float64(401), response["code"])
}

func TestUploadFileMultipart(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	body := &bytes.Buffer{}
	writer := multipart.NewWriter(body)
	
	part, err := writer.CreateFormFile("file", "test.txt")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	_, err = part.Write([]byte("test content"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	writer.WriteField("path", "uploads/test.txt")
	writer.WriteField("mode", "0644")
	writer.Close()
	
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/files", body)
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", writer.FormDataContentType())
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response FileInfo
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "uploads/test.txt", response.Path)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(12), response.Size)
	assert.Equal(t, "0644", response.Mode)
	
	filePath := filepath.Join(tempDir, "uploads", "test.txt")
	content, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "test content", string(content))
}

func TestUploadFileJSON(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	reqBody := UploadFileRequest{
		Path:    "uploads/test.txt",
		Content: base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString([]byte("test content")),
		Mode:    "0644",
	}
	
	body, _ := json.Marshal(reqBody)
	req := httptest.NewRequest("POST", "/api/files", bytes.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response FileInfo
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "uploads/test.txt", response.Path)
	
	filePath := filepath.Join(tempDir, "uploads", "test.txt")
	content, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "test content", string(content))
}

func TestListFiles(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	testDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, "testdir")
	require.NoError(t, os.Mkdir(testDir, 0755))
	
	os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(testDir, "file1.txt"), []byte("content1"), 0644)
	os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(testDir, "file2.txt"), []byte("content2"), 0644)
	
	req := httptest.NewRequest("GET", "/api/files?path=testdir", nil)
	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
	
	var response ListFilesResponse
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Len(t, response.Files, 2)
	
	fileNames := []string{response.Files[0].Name, response.Files[1].Name}
	assert.Contains(t, fileNames, "file1.txt")
	assert.Contains(t, fileNames, "file2.txt")
}

func TestListFilesMissingPath(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+generateValidTestJWT(t))
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	
	server.engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	
	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusBadRequest, w.Code)
	
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &response)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	
	assert.Equal(t, "Missing 'path' query parameter", response["error"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(400), response["code"])
}

func TestListFilesDirectoryNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}

	require.NoError(t, err)

func TestDownloadFile(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	config := &Config{
		Port:      8080,
		Workspace: tempDir,
	}
	
	server, err := NewServer(config)
	require.NoError(t, err)
